docs.title: Welcome to Planck SDK
docs.text: The Planck Python SDK allows you to seamlessly integrate your Python applications with the Planck Quantum Software-as-a-Service (QSaaS) platform. With it, you can design, compile, transpile, simulate, and execute quantum circuits on world-class HPC simulation backends and Quantum Processing Units (QPUs).

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
The SDK requires Python 3.9+.

pip install planck-sdk

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
from planck_sdk.client import PlanckUser

# Initialize the client
client = PlanckUser(api_key="sk_your_api_key_here")

# Run a VQE job with input data
result = client.run(
    data=[0.1, 0.2, 0.5, 0.9],
    algorithm="vqe",
    backend="hpc_gpu"
)

print(result.overview)
result.plot_histogram()

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• End-to-End Execution: Generate and simulate circuits with one call using client.run().
  • Digital Twins: Seamless integration with ML-based error mitigation and recommendations.
  • Detailed Results: Rich result objects ExecutionResult and easy data visualization.

Check out the Client API Reference to get started!

docs.title: planck_sdk.circuit
docs.text:
planck_sdk/circuit.py ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── Quantum circuit data structures for the Planck SDK.

The SDK does NOT build QASM locally. Circuit construction happens server-side in lib/circuit-builder.ts (TypeScript, parametric) with an optional C++ gate- optimiser pass (scripts/transpile_circuit.cpp). The Python SDK sends raw input data to the API and receives a ready-to-run QuantumCircuit in return.

Public classes

CircuitBuildOptions — optional hints the caller may pass to run() QuantumCircuit — server response; immutable after creation

docs.title: CircuitBuildOptions dataclass
docs.text:
Optional parametric hints forwarded to the server-side circuit builder.

All fields are optional — the builder derives sensible defaults from the uploaded dataset when a field is None.

Attributes:

Name Type Description max_qubits Optional[int]

Hard cap on qubit count (2–20). Builder will not exceed this even if the data profile would suggest more.

max_depth Optional[int]

Hint to limit ansatz/QAOA layers so depth stays manageable.

angle_scale float

Multiply all data-derived rotation angles by this factor. Use < 1.0 to damp rotations, > 1.0 to amplify them.

force_layers Optional[int]

Override the auto-computed layer count for VQE / QAOA.

docs.title: to_dict() -> dict
docs.text:
Convert the build options to a dictionary.

Returns:

Name Type Description dict dict

The dictionary representation of the build options.

docs.title: QuantumCircuit dataclass
docs.text:
Immutable representation of a server-generated quantum circuit.

Attributes:

Name Type Description qasm str

OpenQASM 2.0 source (parametric, data-driven).

qubits int

Register size derived from the input data profile.

depth int

Estimated gate depth after optional C++ transpilation.

gate_count int

Total gate operations.

gates List[str]

Parsed gate list (type + qubit indices).

algorithm str

Algorithm key: vqe | qaoa | grover | shor | bell.

recommended_shots int

ML-tuned shot count for statistical accuracy.

param_summary str

Human-readable description of how data shaped the circuit.

transpiled bool

True if the C++ gate-optimiser pass was applied.

docs.title: clean_qasm: str property
docs.text:
Get QASM with comment lines stripped (useful for display).

Returns:

Name Type Description str str

The clean QASM string.

docs.text:
Get string representation of the QuantumCircuit.

Returns:

The string representation.

docs.title: planck_sdk.client
docs.text:
Planck SDK Client - Main interface for interacting with Planck Platform

Install with: pip install planck_sdk

docs.title: PlanckUser
docs.text:
Main user interface for interacting with the Planck Quantum Digital Twins Platform.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default api_key str

Your Planck API key (found in Settings > API Keys)

required base_url Optional[str]

API base URL (default: https://plancktechnologies.xyz)

None timeout int

Request timeout in seconds (default: 60)

60 Example

from planck_sdk import PlanckUser user = PlanckUser(api_key="sk_live_xxx") result = user.run(data=[1,2,3], algorithm="grover") print(result.counts)

docs.title: __repr__() -> str
docs.text:
Get string representation of the PlanckUser.

Returns:

Name Type Description str str

The string representation.

docs.title: generate_circuit(data: Union[List, Dict], algorithm: str = 'vqe', qubits: Optional[int] = None, build_options: Optional[CircuitBuildOptions] = None, sample_count_hint: Optional[int] = None) -> QuantumCircuit
docs.text:
Generate a parametric quantum circuit without executing it.

Circuit angles, register sizes, and layer counts are derived from data by the server-side circuit-builder. Pass build_options to constrain or tune the parametrisation (max qubits, angle scale, etc.).

For large datasets the SDK automatically samples before sending so the payload stays within the API 1 MB / 10 K limit. Pass sample_count_hint to tell the server the true dataset size so circuit scaling (qubits, layers, data_scale) is correct.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default data Union[List, Dict]

Input dataset (list or dict, already sampled).

required algorithm str

'vqe' | 'grover' | 'qaoa' | 'bell' | 'shor'.

'vqe' qubits Optional[int]

Optional qubit count hint (auto-derived if None).

None build_options Optional[CircuitBuildOptions]

Optional CircuitBuildOptions for parametric control.

None sample_count_hint Optional[int]

True row count before sampling (for circuit scaling).

None

Returns:

Type Description QuantumCircuit

QuantumCircuit with data-derived QASM, data_scale, and paramSummary.

docs.title: get_digital_twin(algorithm: str, circuit_info: Dict[str, Any], execution_results: Dict[str, Any], backend_config: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None, input_data: Optional[Any] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]

None

Returns:

Dict with digital twin insights and recommendations

docs.title: list_digital_twins() -> List[Dict[str, Any]]
docs.text:
Return all digital twins belonging to the authenticated user.

Each item in the returned list contains

id — UUID used as digital_twin_id in run() name — human-readable label description — optional description created_at — ISO-8601 timestamp

Example::

twins = user.list_digital_twins()
for t in twins:
    print(t["id"], t["name"])

# Then use an id in run():
result = user.run(data, digital_twin_id=twins[0]["id"])

Returns:

Type Description List[Dict[str, Any]]

List of digital twin dicts, empty list if none exist.

docs.title: list_executions(limit: int = 20, offset: int = 0, status: Optional[str] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]
docs.text:
List past executions for this user.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default limit int

Maximum number of executions to return (1-100, default 20)

20 offset int

Pagination offset (default 0)

0 status Optional[str]

Filter by status ('completed', 'failed', 'running')

None

Returns:

Type Description Dict[str, Any]

Dict with 'executions' list, 'total' count, 'limit', 'offset'.

docs.title: run(data: Union[List, Dict, str], algorithm: str = 'vqe', shots: Optional[int] = None, backend: str = 'auto', error_mitigation: str = 'auto', circuit_name: Optional[str] = None, target_latency: Optional[int] = None, qubits: Optional[int] = None, digital_twin_id: Optional[str] = None, build_options: Optional[CircuitBuildOptions] = None, wait: bool = True, scenario_name: Optional[str] = None, scenario_type: Optional[str] = None, objective: Optional[str] = None, risk_tolerance: Optional[str] = None, execution_strategy: Optional[str] = None) -> ExecutionResult
docs.text:
Generate and execute a parametric quantum circuit from input data.

The circuit is built server-side by lib/circuit-builder.ts: gate angles, register sizes, and layer counts are all derived from the uploaded dataset. Pass build_options (CircuitBuildOptions) to override defaults such as max qubit count, angle scaling, or layer count.

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default data Union[List, Dict, str]

Input data (list, dict, or file path to CSV/JSON).

required algorithm str

'vqe' | 'grover' | 'qaoa' | 'bell' | 'shor'.

'vqe' shots Optional[int]

Measurement shots (auto-tuned by RL when None).

None backend str

'auto' | 'quantum_inspired_gpu' | 'hpc_gpu' | 'quantum_qpu'.

'auto' error_mitigation str

'auto' | 'none' | 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'.

'auto' circuit_name Optional[str]

Optional label for the execution log.

None target_latency Optional[int]

Latency hint in ms (affects backend selection).

None qubits Optional[int]

Qubit hint — builder may exceed if data requires it.

None digital_twin_id Optional[str]

ID of a digital twin to link this execution to. Pass None, 0, or '' to leave unlinked (default). Use list_digital_twins() to see available IDs.

None build_options Optional[CircuitBuildOptions]

Fine-grained parametric hints (CircuitBuildOptions).

None wait bool

Block until execution completes (default: True).

True scenario_name Optional[str]

Optional human-readable label for this scenario (e.g. 'Peak Load 2026').

None scenario_type Optional[str]

One of 'Baseline' | 'Stress test' | 'Optimization' | 'Risk analysis' | 'Custom'.

None objective Optional[str]

One of 'minimize_runtime' | 'maximize_reliability' | 'minimize_cost' | 'maximize_accuracy' | 'balanced'.

None risk_tolerance Optional[str]

One of 'conservative' | 'balanced' | 'aggressive'.

None execution_strategy Optional[str]

One of 'single' | 'batch' | 'compare'.

None

Returns:

Type Description ExecutionResult

ExecutionResult with counts, fidelity, and ML tuning metadata.

docs.title: simulate(qasm: str, shots: int = 1024, backend: str = 'auto', error_mitigation: str = 'medium', circuit_name: Optional[str] = None, algorithm: str = 'vqe', qubits: Optional[int] = None) -> ExecutionResult
docs.text:
Execute a raw QASM circuit directly (skip generate-circuit step).

Parameters:

Name Type Description Default qasm str

OpenQASM 2.0 code to execute

required shots int

Number of measurement shots (1-100000)

1024 backend str

Execution backend ('auto', 'quantum_inspired_gpu', 'hpc_gpu', 'quantum_qpu')

'auto' error_mitigation str

Error mitigation level ('auto', 'none', 'low', 'medium', 'high'). 'auto' uses RL-driven selection based on historical executions.

'medium' circuit_name Optional[str]

Optional name for the execution

None algorithm str

Algorithm label for logging ('vqe', 'grover', 'qaoa', 'qft', 'bell', 'shor')

'vqe' qubits Optional[int]

Number of qubits (auto-extracted from QASM if None)

None

Returns:

Type Description ExecutionResult

ExecutionResult with counts, fidelity, error_mitigation, ml_tuning, and metadata.

Example

result = user.simulate( ... qasm='OPENQASM 2.0; include "qelib1.inc"; qreg q[2]; creg c[2]; h q[0]; cx q[0],q[1]; measure q -> c;', ... shots=2048, ... ) print(result.counts)

docs.title: transpile(qasm: str, backend: str = 'quantum_qpu', qubits: Optional[int] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]

docs.title: planck_sdk.result
docs.text: Planck SDK - Execution Result representation

docs.title: ExecutionResult dataclass
docs.text:
Represents the result of a quantum circuit execution.

Attributes:

Name Type Description execution_id Optional[str]

Unique identifier for this execution

counts Dict[str, int]

Measurement counts for each basis state

success_rate float

Success rate percentage

runtime_ms float

Execution runtime in milliseconds

memory List[str]

List of measurement outcomes

circuit Optional[QuantumCircuit]

The executed QuantumCircuit (if available)

backend str

Effective backend that was actually used (resolved by policy)

shots int

Number of shots executed

algorithm str

Algorithm type

backend_reason Optional[str]

Human-readable explanation of why this backend was chosen

backend_hint Optional[str]

The backend the user originally requested (None if 'auto')

docs.title: analysis: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] property
docs.text:
Digital Twin analysis: behavior insights, data patterns, topology.

Returns:

Type Description Optional[Dict[str, Any]]

Optional[Dict[str, Any]]: The digital twin analysis, or None if unavailable.

docs.title: fidelity: float property
docs.text:
Estimated fidelity based on success rate.

Returns:

Name Type Description float float

The fidelity value, calculated as success_rate / 100.

docs.title: most_frequent: str property
docs.text:
Return the most frequently measured state.

Returns:

Name Type Description str str

The state string with the highest measurement count.

docs.title: overview: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] property
docs.text:

Returns:

Type Description Optional[Dict[str, Any]]

Optional[Dict[str, Any]]: The digital twin overview, or None if unavailable.

docs.title: performance: Optional[Dict[str, str]] property
docs.text:

Returns:

Type Description Optional[Dict[str, str]]

Optional[Dict[str, str]]: Performance metrics dictionary, or None if unavailable.

docs.title: probabilities: Dict[str, float] property

Return measurement probabilities for each state.

Returns:

Type Description Dict[str, float]

Dict[str, float]: A dictionary mapping states to their probability.

docs.title: recommendations: Optional[List[str]] property
docs.text:
Digital Twin system recommendations.

Returns:

Type Description Optional[List[str]]

Optional[List[str]]: A list of recommendations, or None if unavailable.
